why: Tier3 TV feasible=False; n_eff_q25_tv=122 vs required≈252 (tv_window≈84)
Tier3 STATIC feasible=True; n_eff_q25_static=139 vs required≈82
Non-stationarity is not a blocker if TIME_VARYING_gVAR is feasible; it mainly affects STATIC feasibility.
overall.technical_summary: Tier3_LaggedDynamicNetwork with STATIC_gVAR is recommended and FullyReady (97.3/100). Ready vars: 5 of 14; soft_drops=9; hard_drop=0. Time: regular_or_quasi_regular; irregular_fraction=0; duplicates=0. STATIC: n_eff_q25=139 (≥82 required); stationarity_required=true; feasible. TV: not feasible (n_eff_q25_tv=122; tv_window≈84; tv_required≈252). Non-stationarity flags exist for multiple vars (P01, P02, P04, P05, P07, P08, C02, C05, C06) per ADF/KPSS tests, driving exclusion from STATIC for some vars; but time-varying gVAR could be viable with larger dataset to support smoothing/windows. Missingness base ~10.8%; data is well-ordered and ready for the chosen tier. Overall, evidence supports Tier3_STATIC_gVAR given current data; TIME_VARYING_gVAR remains a future option with more points and smoothing capacity.
overall.client_friendly_summary: Your data is almost ready for a dynamic network model, using a static lagged approach. Five of the fourteen candidate variables are ready for analysis now. The timing is regular and the data quality is good, but several variables show non-stationary patterns, which currently limit switching to a time-varying model. If you collect more data and keep timing consistent, you could later move toward a time-varying approach for potentially richer insights.
next_steps: Prioritize data collection for the remaining candidate variables to improve stability.
Review and, if possible, preprocess non-stationary variables to improve STATIC_gVAR readiness.
Increase total data points to enable future TIME_VARYING_gVAR feasibility.
Maintain strict time regularity to minimize irregularities and missingness.
Reduce missing data where feasible to further boost the missingness score.
Reassess stationarity tests after additional data collection to re-evaluate viability of TIME_VARYING_gVAR.
caveats: Several variables show non-stationarity under STATIC_gVAR assumptions, driving soft_drops.
STATIC lagged gVAR assumes approximate stationarity; trends may bias results if not mitigated.
TIME_VARYING_gVAR remains infeasible now due to insufficient effective points (tv_required_points ≈ 252).
Low effective sample per variable under some configurations could affect reliability.
Non-stationarity tests (ADF/KPSS) may be sensitive to windowing and preprocessing choices.
High collinearity or near-linear dependencies could inflate n_eff estimates and affect interpretability.
Missingness around 10.8% may still influence parameter estimates if not addressed.
Model readiness depends on careful windowing and smoothing choices for any potential time-varying analysis.

nonstationarity_handling.statement: Non-stationarity is not a problem if TIME_VARYING_gVAR is feasible; it mainly limits STATIC_gVAR feasibility.
